generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OperationalParameterSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous sensor component for real-time acquisition of operational parameters in the physical manufacturing robot axis system, enabling contextual data traces for digital shadow representation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalParameterDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous sensor component for real-time monitoring of environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, etc.) in the manufacturing automation context."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
]

instance PositionFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-frequency sensor providing real-time position feedback for the robotic axis system, critical for closed-loop control and digital twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionFeedbackDataTransmitted
]

instance TorqueFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-frequency sensor measuring torque values for the permanent magnet synchronous motor in the MABI MAX 100 robotic axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueFeedbackDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OperationalParameterDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalParameterSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

instance PositionFeedbackDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionFeedbackSensor
]

instance TorqueFeedbackDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TorqueFeedbackSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ContextualDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted contextual data traces derived from physical sensor inputs, enabling purpose-specific analytics in the manufacturing automation domain."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalParameterDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted, PositionFeedbackDataTransmitted, TorqueFeedbackDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationBackend
]

instance FrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model representing friction characteristics in the MABI MAX 100 robotic axis, implemented via MathWorks Simulink for dynamic system simulation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine
]

instance BehavioralStateMachine : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 state machine model representing the dynamic behavior of robotic motion and control logic for the manufacturing axis system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicValidator
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing historical operational data traces for diagnostics and performance analysis of the robotic axis system."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalParameterDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AnalyticsToolchain
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataAggregationBackend : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Scalable storage solution for contextual data traces aggregation, supporting one-way data flow from physical sensors to digital representation."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataProcessingService
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment executing dynamic system simulations for friction analysis and control algorithm validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance ControlLogicValidator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML state machine validation tool for behavioral modeling of robotic motion and control logic."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlValidationService
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message Queuing Telemetry Transport broker enabling low-latency, reliable communication between physical sensors and digital twin data aggregation layer."
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorDataIngestionService
]

instance AnalyticsToolchain : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous toolchain integrating MATLAB, general-purpose code (C++, Java), and specialized analytics tools for predictive analytics and performance optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables DecisionSupportService
]

instance SysMLModelRepository : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram repository maintaining component-connector models of the MABI MAX 100 system architecture."
    DTDFVocab:enables ArchitectureValidationService
]

// C6: Services
instance SensorDataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service responsible for automated one-way data collection from physical sensors to digital twin, ensuring real-time aggregation for active DT components."
    DTDFVocab:provides SensorDataQualityInsight
]

instance DataProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling aggregation and abstraction of contextual data traces for purpose-specific manufacturing analytics."
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessOptimizationInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

instance ControlValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating robotic motion control logic through SysML state machine simulations and behavioral modeling."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlPerformanceInsight
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating actionable recommendations through contextual data aggregation and predictive analytics for manufacturing decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides ManufacturingOptimizationAction
]

instance ArchitectureValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring interface consistency and composition rules through SysML IBD modeling of the robotic system architecture."
    DTDFVocab:provides ArchitectureComplianceInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions

    base:desc "Insight providing confidence measures for sensor data quality based on real-time acquisition and uncertainty quantification in digital twin fidelity."
]

]

]

]

    base:desc "Automated action recommendations for manufacturing process optimization based on predictive analytics and decision support models."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ArchitectureComplianceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight verifying compliance with SysML interface consistency and composition rules in the robotic system architecture."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance JointLevelMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded controller interfacing with joint-level motors in the MABI MAX 100 robotic axis, implementing closed-loop actuation with maximum working range [-90°, 90°] and peak speed 75°/s."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorControlService
]

    base:desc "Safety enforcement mechanism for the robotic system, preventing collisions and operational limit violations through embedded safety protocols."

]

]

    base:desc "Insight assessing the performance of motor actuation based on closed-loop control feedback and predicted state translations."
]

]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Precision manufacturing automation environment containing the MABI MAX 100 robotic axis system and its associated physical components."

]

instance MABIMAX100RoboticAxis : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical manufacturing robot axis (MABI MAX 100) comprising permanent magnet synchronous motor, gearboxes, and Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller for friction modeling and control."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the document operates with **real-time, data-driven representations** of physical systems, specifically focusing on **live data collection during the operation of an industrial robot** (e.g., MABI MAX). While explicit synchronization frequencies or temporal granularity requirements are not detailed, the system relies on **continuous sensor data streaming** (e.g., via MQTT or similar protocols) to maintain alignment between the physical and digital domains. Behavioral modeling tools like **MathWorks Simulink** or other simulation methods are integrated to enforce **dynamic updates** of the digital model based on real-time inputs, ensuring temporal consistency for operational decision-making. Latency requirements are implicitly implied by the need for **live data-driven representations**, though no specific thresholds (e.g., sub-millisecond, millisecond, or second-level granularity) are quantified in the provided context."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration and coordination are framed within a **heterogeneous toolchain** integrating **architecture description languages (ADLs)** (e.g., SysML IBDs, MontiArc) with **behavioral modeling tools** (e.g., Simulink, state machines, or general-purpose simulation methods). The **system-wide coordination** is achieved through **model-based integration**, where components are described via **component & connector perspectives** (e.g., interfaces, interconnections, and composition) and behavior is modeled via **state machines or automata**. Resource management is implicitly handled by the **toolchain’s ability to aggregate and abstract data traces** (as per digital shadow definitions in Kritzinger et al. [32]), though no explicit **orchestration patterns** (e.g., event-driven, publish-subscribe) or **resource allocation strategies** are detailed. The document emphasizes the need for **interoperability across heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives**, suggesting that coordination relies on **shared modeling notations** and **tool interoperability standards** rather than explicit system-wide governance."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which decompose system modeling into hierarchical abstraction layers: **requirements**, **functional architecture**, **logical architecture**, and **product/technical layer**. Transition management between these stages is facilitated by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, and **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition rules. Behavioral modeling is further refined using **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **MontiArc Automaton**, while detailed simulations leverage tools like **MathWorks Simulink** or general-purpose languages (e.g., **C++/Java**). Lifecycle governance involves integrating heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives and ensuring interoperability across disparate modeling notations and tools, as referenced in [31]. The lifecycle also includes **abstraction and aggregation** of contextual data traces (as per the Digital Shadow reference model in [32]), though the DT itself introduces **active components** for bidirectional data flow, contrasting with passive Digital Shadow systems. No explicit lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are mentioned in the provided documents.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity and validity** through:
- **Model fidelity requirements**: Ensured via **multi-layered MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** to maintain consistency across abstraction layers,
- **Validation and verification methods**: Implicitly supported by **SysML state machines**, **MontiArc Automaton**, and **external simulation tools (e.g., Simulink)** for behavioral validation,
- **Uncertainty sources and quantification**: Addressed via **data aggregation and abstraction** mechanisms (as per Digital Shadow Reference Model) to mitigate noise in contextual traces,
- **Error detection and correction mechanisms**: Not explicitly detailed, but inferred to rely on **active DT components** (e.g., software systems interpreting incoming data) for real-time adjustments,
- **Quality assurance processes**: Enforced through **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **interoperability frameworks** to ensure alignment between models, tools, and operational data.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s deployment infrastructure is implicitly referenced through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** tools and methodologies, such as **SysML** and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** for logical/physical architecture modeling. The system may utilize **visualization dashboards** (e.g., for monitoring or analysis) and **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** for digital representation, though explicit hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise), computational/storage requirements, or orchestration mechanisms (e.g., Kubernetes) are not detailed in the provided documents. The deployment is framed within a **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** context, requiring compatibility across multiple modeling notations and tools."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The provided documents reference **industry standards** and **interoperability frameworks** indirectly through **MBSE methodologies** and **ADLs**:
- **Modeling Languages**: **SysML** ([17]), **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc** ([23]–[27]) for architecture description.
- **Simulation Tools**: **MathWorks Simulink** ([28]) and other tools ([29], [30]) for behavioral modeling.
- **Interoperability**: The documents emphasize **integration of heterogeneous tools** ([31]) and **stakeholder perspectives**, implying adherence to **open standards** (e.g., **OMG’s SysML**, **SAE’s AADL**) for modularity and exchangeability. However, no explicit **certification requirements** (e.g., ISO/IEC 15288, IEC 62264) or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., **IEC 61508 for safety**) are cited. The **Digital Shadow reference model** ([32]) does not extend to DT-specific standards, and no mention of **semantic interoperability** (e.g., **ontologies**, **OWL/SWRL**) or **data exchange protocols** (e.g., **OPC UA**, **ROS 2**) is provided.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates **physical safety mechanisms** through the **safety cell** of the robotic system, which restricts the maximum working range of the robotic arm to **[-90°, 90°]**, ensuring operator and equipment protection within predefined operational boundaries. The system leverages **friction modeling** (as described in [45]) to enhance **motion accuracy** and **stability**, indirectly contributing to safety by mitigating unintended deviations during dynamic operations. However, explicit **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **authentication systems** are not documented in the provided sources. The Digital Twin architecture does not reference **Functional Mockup Interface (FMI)** or **MQTT-based communication** (despite [52] mentioning MQTT in general IoT contexts), nor does it detail **risk assessment frameworks**, **fail-safe mechanisms**, or **safety-critical system considerations** beyond the physical safety cell constraints. The focus of the documents remains on **model-driven engineering** ([43]), **digital shadows** ([42]), and **robot dynamics** ([45]), with no explicit mention of data security, privacy compliance (e.g., GDPR), or consent management."
]
